posts.title: Polls Expanded: More APC vs PDP Comparisons for 2027 Conversations (No Fighting Please)
posts.excerpt: We expanded poll packs so Nigerians can test long-term party performance sentiment with less noise and more structure.
posts.featured_image_alt: Chart and ballot illustration for evote.ng polls update
posts.meta_description: evote.ng expands APC vs PDP comparison polls to support structured 2027 election conversations and citizen sentiment tracking across Nigeria.
posts.meta_keywords: evote.ng polls, APC vs PDP, 2027 election Nigeria, political sentiment
posts.body_html:
If Nigerian politics was only about vibes, WhatsApp groups for don settle am since.

But because everybody has an opinion (and volume), we expanded our poll packs so you can compare party performance sentiment in a more structured way.

What changed

  • More comparison questions for long-term governance outcomes.
  • Cleaner phrasing to reduce misunderstandings.
  • Better grouping so you can answer quickly without getting tired.

Remember: polls are not prophecy. They’re a mirror. Sometimes that mirror go show you things wey you no ready for — but that’s how accountability starts.

posts.title: More Officials and Agencies Added: Your Search Just Got Longer (Sorry, Not Sorry)
posts.excerpt: We expanded profile coverage across tiers so citizens can find and rate more public offices with clearer role context.
posts.featured_image_alt: Directory and profile cards illustration for public officials
posts.meta_description: evote.ng adds more Nigerian officials and agency entities to improve discoverability and support citizen ratings across federal, state and local tiers.
posts.meta_keywords: public officials Nigeria, agency profiles, evote.ng officials list
posts.body_html:
We added more officials and agencies to the public pages — because accountability no suppose stop at only the popular names.

Why it matters

  • More people can find the right office faster.
  • Ratings become more representative across sectors.
  • Communities can focus on the offices that affect them daily.

Translation: if your area has been shouting “who is in charge?” you now have a better chance of actually finding who is in charge.

posts.title: Role-Based Rating Templates Tightened: We No Dey Rate Minister Like LGA Chairman Again
posts.excerpt: We refined rating criteria so scoring aligns with each office mandate—less generic ‘good/bad’, more useful feedback.
posts.featured_image_alt: Checklist and sector icons illustration for role-based ratings
posts.meta_description: evote.ng improves role-based rating templates so Nigerians can score ministers, governors, agencies, and local officials with mandate-specific criteria.
posts.meta_keywords: role based ratings, rate public officials, Nigeria accountability, evote.ng templates
posts.body_html:
One of the fastest ways to spoil ratings is to use the same yardstick for everybody.

You can’t rate a health commissioner like a DISCO. You can’t rate a governor like a market manager.

What we improved

  • Cleaner criteria per role and sector.
  • Less generic scoring, more mandate-driven feedback.
  • Better signals for comparing similar offices.

This makes your reviews more actionable — and harder to dismiss as “online noise”.

posts.title: WhatsApp Sharing for Official Profiles Is Live: Now the Group Chat Can Do Something Useful
posts.excerpt: You can now share an official’s profile link to WhatsApp—help your community coordinate evidence-based feedback.
posts.featured_image_alt: Share icon and chat bubbles illustration for WhatsApp sharing
posts.meta_description: evote.ng enables WhatsApp sharing for official profile pages so Nigerian communities can coordinate ratings, evidence, and follow-ups more easily.
posts.meta_keywords: WhatsApp sharing, civic tech Nigeria, evote.ng profile link
posts.body_html:
You know that moment when somebody drops one “who is our commissioner sef?” inside the group chat, and the debate starts like World Cup?

Now you can share the actual profile link directly to WhatsApp. Less argument. More facts.

Use it well

  • Share the profile.
  • Share the issue with evidence (photo/date/location).
  • Rate and review with specifics.

Community gist is good. Community evidence is better.

posts.title: Homepage Now Highlights Key Leader Scores: Faster Discovery, Less Scrolling Wahala
posts.excerpt: We improved homepage discovery so users can quickly find high-impact profiles and submit informed reviews.
posts.featured_image_alt: Homepage cards illustration featuring leader scores
posts.meta_description: evote.ng updates the homepage to spotlight key Nigerian leader scores for faster discovery and more informed citizen ratings.
posts.meta_keywords: evote.ng homepage, leader scores, governance rankings Nigeria
posts.body_html:
Sometimes you enter a site and you’re like, “Where should I start?”

We improved the homepage to highlight key leader scores so discovery is faster, and your energy is spent on rating—not on hunting.

Why it matters

  • Faster access to high-impact profiles.
  • More visibility for comparisons and trends.
  • More informed reviews from more people.

Because if accountability is hard to find, people will abandon it.

posts.title: Power-Sector Entities Added: Rate Service Quality, Not Just Your Anger
posts.excerpt: Power institutions now appear with utility-specific criteria so citizens can score reliability, billing fairness and response time.
posts.featured_image_alt: Electricity tower and meter illustration for power sector
posts.meta_description: evote.ng adds power-sector entities with specialized rating criteria so Nigerians can rate electricity reliability, billing fairness, and customer service quality.
posts.meta_keywords: rate disco, electricity Nigeria, power sector accountability, evote.ng power
posts.body_html:
We understand. Power matter fits turn gentle person to comedian and preacher at the same time.

But to get accountability, we need structured feedback, not only “these people!”

What you can now rate with clarity

  • Outage frequency and restoration speed
  • Billing fairness and dispute handling
  • Communication and customer support
  • Safety issues and response time

Rate the service experience you can describe. That’s how complaints become signals.

posts.title: Governor Visibility Improved in Rankings: Now You Can Compare Without Doing Research Like Thesis
posts.excerpt: We improved how governors show up in featured rankings to help residents compare service delivery perception in one place.
posts.featured_image_alt: Map of Nigeria with ranking badges illustration
posts.meta_description: evote.ng improves governor visibility in rankings so Nigerians can compare state governance perception and submit reviews more easily.
posts.meta_keywords: governor ranking Nigeria, state governance, evote.ng leaderboard
posts.body_html:
When you want compare states, sometimes it feels like you need coffee, laptop, and one cousin wey dey Abuja.

We improved governor visibility in featured rankings so you can compare faster and focus on what matters: service outcomes.

Use it to check perceptions, then read written feedback for the full story.

posts.title: Expanded LGA Coverage: Because Local Government Is Where Your Daily Wahala Lives
posts.excerpt: Broader LGA coverage helps communities track sanitation, roads, water and PHC performance closer to home.
posts.featured_image_alt: Local government building and community services illustration
posts.meta_description: evote.ng expands local government coverage so Nigerians can rate LGA chairmen and track community services like sanitation, roads, water and primary healthcare.
posts.meta_keywords: LGA chairman Nigeria, local government accountability, evote.ng LGA
posts.body_html:
If you want see governance in real life, don’t start from Abuja. Start from your street.

We expanded LGA coverage so communities can track what affects them daily—drainage, refuse, markets, roads, and PHC.

The goal is simple: make feedback closer to where life happens.

posts.title: Rating Criteria Expanded: More Signals, Less ‘Vibes Only’ Reviews
posts.excerpt: We expanded criteria to capture security, economy, education, health, and sector-specific outcomes more clearly.
posts.featured_image_alt: Radar chart illustration showing multiple governance signals
posts.meta_description: evote.ng expands rating criteria to capture stronger governance signals like security, economy, education, health, and service delivery outcomes across Nigeria.
posts.meta_keywords: governance rating criteria, Nigeria accountability, evote.ng reviews
posts.body_html:
When ratings are too generic, everybody ends up writing the same thing: “Good” or “Bad”.

We expanded criteria so reviews can capture the real issues Nigerians talk about: security, cost of living pressure, schools, health access, and sector service quality.

More signals means better comparisons—and better pressure for improvement.

posts.title: How We Curate News Without Chasing Clout: The evote.ng Watchlist Method
posts.excerpt: Our news page focuses on governance-relevant updates and practical context—less panic, more ‘why it matters’ for citizens.
posts.featured_image_alt: Magnifying glass and checklist illustration for curated governance news
posts.meta_description: Learn how evote.ng curates governance news for Nigerians: what we watch, how we summarise, and why it matters for citizen ratings and polls.
posts.meta_keywords: curated governance news Nigeria, evote.ng news, civic tech editorial
posts.body_html:
Let’s not lie: Nigerian internet likes drama. If you post calm analysis, people will ask, “Where is the shock?”

But for accountability work, we prefer signal over noise.

Our watchlist method

  • Relevance: does it affect service delivery, rights, or public money?
  • Clarity: can we explain it in plain language?
  • Actionability: can citizens rate, ask questions, or follow up?

Sometimes the suspense is not in the headline. It’s in the follow-up: what changed on ground after the announcement?
